seoTitle: Build Better Reports With Power BI's New Card Visual
metaDescription: Learn how to set up and customize Power BI's new Card visual to highlight key metrics clearly, plus tips for using it in real dashboards.
intro: The new Card visual in Power BI gives you more design flexibility than the old single-number card, making it easier to spotlight the metrics decision-makers check first. This guide covers setting up the visual, customizing its look, and applying it to real business dashboards. It's aimed at report builders who want cleaner, more scannable KPI displays.

steps.heading: Set up the new Card visual
steps.body: Add the Card visual to your report canvas and assign the field or measure you want to display as the headline metric.

steps.heading: Customize the Card visual
steps.body: Adjust formatting options like font size, color, and layout to make the metric stand out and match your report's overall design.

steps.heading: Apply it to business use cases
steps.body: Use Card visuals to highlight metrics like total sales, project completion percentage, or active customer count where a single glance should tell the story.

steps.heading: Follow tips for effective reporting
steps.body: Group related Card visuals together and pair them with trend indicators or comparison values so viewers get context, not just a bare number.

faq.question: What's different about the new Card visual compared to the old one?
faq.answer: The new Card visual offers more customization for layout, icons, and comparison values, giving report builders more design flexibility than the classic single-value card.

faq.question: Can the Card visual show a trend alongside the main number?
faq.answer: Yes, depending on the configuration, you can add comparison or trend indicators to give context to the primary metric shown on the card.

faq.question: Is the new Card visual available in all Power BI versions?
faq.answer: Availability depends on your Power BI Desktop version, since newer visual features roll out gradually, so check for updates if you don't see it yet.

faq.question: How many Card visuals should I put on one dashboard?
faq.answer: Keep it focused — a handful of well-chosen Card visuals highlighting the most important metrics reads better than crowding a page with too many.
